title: dalbergia hygrophila
intro: Dalbergia hygrophila, also known as the Indian rosewood or kala lagu, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In Ayurvedic medicine, Dalbergia hygrophila is typically prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is also used in herbal preparations such as oils and ghee.
